latency_thres: Stability threshold (assumed unstable if avg latency exceeds this).
k: Network radix (number of nodes per dimension).
n: Number of dimensions in the topology.
c: Concentration (number of end nodes attached per router).
routing_function: Name of the routing function to use (e.g., 'west_first', 'none' for default).
subnets: Number of physical sub-networks (virtual networks) in the system.
use_noc_latency: Enable noc latency calculation to adjust for node/router placement (1 = yes).
channel_file: File containing per-link channel lengths for heterogeneous latency modeling.
num_vcs: Number of virtual channels per physical port.
vc_buf_size: Buffer size per virtual channel in flits (for private buffer policy).
buf_size: Total shared buffer size in flits (if buffer_policy is shared; -1 = unlimited).
alloc_iters: Number of iterations for iterative allocators (e.g., iSLIP).
input_speedup: Input port expansion factor (number of internal input ports per physical port).
output_speedup: Output port expansion factor (number of internal output ports per physical port).
vc_alloc_delay: Pipeline stages for virtual channel allocation.
st_final_delay: Pipeline stages for switch traversal and link traversal.
speculative: Enable speculative switch allocation (0 = off, 1 = on).
injection_rate: Packet injection rate per node (packets per node per cycle); can be specified as a string for vector specification (per class or per node).
packet_size: Number of flits per packet; can be specified as a string for vector specification (per class).
burst_alpha: Burst interval parameter (for bursty process): probability of switching from OFF to ON.
burst_beta: Burst length parameter (for bursty process): probability of switching from ON to OFF.
use_read_write: Use read/write request/reply scheme (0 = no, 1 = yes); can be set via string for vector specification.
print_activity: Print detailed router and channel activity.
watch_out: File to write watched flit information.
watch_flits: Comma-separated list of flit IDs to track.
watch_packets: Comma-separated list of packet IDs to track.
tech_file: Path to the technology parameters file.
channel_width: Width of the physical links in bits.
x: Number of routers in the X dimension (for mesh/torus).
y: Number of routers in the Y dimension (for mesh/torus).
xr: Number of nodes per router in the X dimension (only if concentration c > 1).
yr: Number of nodes per router in the Y dimension (only if concentration c > 1).
link_failures: Legacy: number of link failures to inject (0 = none).
fail_seed: Seed for link failure injection; set to integer or 'time' to use current time.
in_ports: Number of input ports per router.
out_ports: Number of output ports per router.
output_delay: Pipeline delay cycles at the output stage.
credit_delay: Pipeline delay cycles for credit return.
internal_speedup: Internal speedup factor (allows faster internal crossbar traversal; >1 means multiple flits can be transferred internally per cycle).
output_buffer_size: Size of output buffers in flits (-1 = unlimited).
spec_check_elig: During speculation, check if VC is eligible for allocation (1 = yes).
spec_check_cred: During speculation, check if VC has sufficient credit (1 = yes).
spec_mask_by_reqs: Mask speculative requests by actual requests (0 = no, 1 = yes).
spec_sw_allocator: Switch allocator type used for speculative allocation (e.g., 'prio').
private_bufs: Number of private buffers (if >=0); -1 means default (all VCs private?).
private_buf_size: Size of each private buffer in flits (used when private_bufs >=0); can be set via string for vector specification.
private_buf_start_vc: Starting VC index for private buffer allocation (-1 = default); can be set via string for vector specification.
private_buf_end_vc: Ending VC index for private buffer allocation (-1 = default); can be set via string for vector specification.
max_held_slots: Maximum number of time slots a flit can hold the bus? (unused or legacy).
feedback_aging_scale: Scaling factor for feedback aging in allocators (to prioritize older requests).
wait_for_tail_credit: Wait for tail credit before reallocating a VC (0 = no, 1 = yes).
vc_busy_when_full: Mark VCs as busy when they have no credit available (0 = no, 1 = yes).
vc_prioritize_empty: Prioritize empty VCs over non-empty ones in VC allocation (0 = no, 1 = yes).
vc_priority_donation: Allow high-priority flits to donate priority to low-priority flits queued behind (0 = no, 1 = yes).
vc_shuffle_requests: Shuffle VC allocator requests to avoid unfairness (0 = no, 1 = yes).
hold_switch_for_packet: Hold switch configuration for the entire packet (0 = no, 1 = yes).
st_prepare_delay: Pipeline stages for switch traversal preparation.
vct: Enable virtual channel tracing (0 = off, 1 = on).
classes: Number of traffic classes (for class-based priority).
class_priority: Class priority scheme (0 = disable, 1 = enable); can be set via string for vector specification per class.
perm_seed: Seed value for random permutation traffic pattern generator; can be set to 'time'.
injection_rate_uses_flits: If 1, injection rate measured in flits per cycle instead of packets.
packet_size_rate: If multiple values per class, set probabilities for each (weight for distribution); can be specified as a string for vector specification.
batch_size: Batch traffic manager batch size (number of packets per batch).
max_outstanding_requests: Maximum outstanding requests (0 = unlimited) for read/write traffic.
write_fraction: Fraction of requests that are writes (vs reads); can be set via string for vector specification.
